executive_conclusion: Commit 9637e08 materially corrects the three original DDL-boundary blockers (F-01 legacy backfill, F-02 cod_rt_norm contract, F-03 assignment supersede) and also closes F-05, F-06, F-07, F-09, F-10, F-11. The resolved view now consumes only v_ruta_rutero_latest_week_batch_v2 (one batch per week) AND excludes legacy_inferred for any week that has an ACTIVE assignment (keyed on the WEEK, not the batch id), so the 70 bajas can no longer be backfilled; a new postcheck additionally compares the resolved grain set to the assigned batch grain set (missing/extra must be 0). The view exposes both cod_rt and cod_rt_norm, reconciling the loader/postcheck contract while staying compatible with refresh (f.cod_rt). The apply now acquires a per-week lock, fetches the ACTIVE assignment FOR UPDATE, blocks an idempotent re-apply, supersedes the prior ACTIVE row, and stores replaces_ruta_batch_id, so the retroactive replacement cycle is implementable. The resolved logical_rank ORDER BY no longer uses source_row (it uses weekly obligation, day-plan sum, operational completeness, normalized operational tuple, then row_hash), satisfying BD-CG005B-001 (now APPROVED) and the source_row-independence constraint. One canonical surface hash (SHA-256 over sorted source_row|row_hash) is now computed identically in Python and over DB-fetched rows and is compared in postcheck/idempotency/rollback; a canonical resolved-surface hash is computed by the same function in plan and postcheck. TWO corrections remain before a trustworthy apply/rollback: (F-08) the new advisory lock calls pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...), hashtextextended(...)) with two bigint arguments, but PostgreSQL only provides pg_advisory_xact_lock(bigint) and pg_advisory_xact_lock(integer,integer); int8 does not implicitly cast to int4, so this two-bigint call has no matching overload and will raise at apply/rollback time (the mocked tests cannot catch it); and (F-04) the rollback now genuinely restores public.ruta_rutero from the previous batch history (distinct on row_hash), reactivates the prior assignment, and marks the failed one ROLLED_BACK, but it commits WITHOUT a full postcheck (no assertion that the restored surface hash equals the previous assignment's stored hash, and no revalidation of the weekly/resolved views or grain sets before commit). Because the three original blockers are cleared, the unit is no longer BLOCKED_BEFORE_DDL: it can advance to an isolated local PostgreSQL lab to apply the DDL, run dry-run, and behaviorally prove F-01/F-02/F-03/F-05/F-06/F-07. However it is VALIDATED_WITH_CORRECTIONS, not clean, because the advisory-lock signature must be fixed before any apply succeeds and the rollback must gain a full pre-commit postcheck before it can be trusted.

loader run_weekly_replacement_rollback 1581-1693 now executes: advisory lock, current-hash check (1612-1614), lock current ACTIVE assignment FOR UPDATE, read replaces_ruta_batch_id, lock previous SUPERSEDED assignment, delete public + restore_public_from_history(previous batch), mark failed ROLLED_BACK, reactivate previous ACTIVE, commit.
loader restore_public_from_history 965-1015 uses 'select distinct on (row_hash)' ordered by row_hash, source_row -> exact-duplicate exclusion.

GAP: after restore (1660) it computes restored_summary (1661) but never asserts restored hash equals the previous assignment's stored current_surface_hash; no re-run of run_postcheck or weekly/resolved-view/grain-set checks before conn.commit (1682).
tests 54/55 only assert source substrings ('ROLLED_BACK','ACTIVE','restore_public_from_history','distinct on (row_hash)'); no behavioral or full-postcheck assertion.
validation_5_f04_rollback.required_correction: Before the rollback commit, assert the restored current_surface_hash equals the previous assignment's stored current_surface_hash, and revalidate the weekly view (explicit assignment now points to the reactivated batch), the resolved view, and the resolved-vs-assigned grain set. Do not commit a rollback that only restores rows and updates statuses.

validation_6_f05_f06_hashes.db_current_hash_evidence: fetch_current_snapshot_summary (loader 1070-1084) selects source_row,row_hash from public.ruta_rutero ordered and calls the SAME canonical_current_surface_hash, so the DB-side hash is byte-comparable to the Python plan hash (prior F-06 MD5/row_hash-only mismatch removed).
validation_6_f05_f06_hashes.resolved_hash_evidence: canonical_resolved_surface_hash (loader 636-655) is used in the plan (resolved_surface_rows_from_current, ~710) and in run_postcheck (1454-1458) over fetch_resolved_surface_rows -> identical columns and normalization.

evidence: sql/11:236-248 logical_rank ORDER BY: EXPLICIT first, visitas_exigidas desc, day-plan sum desc, operational_completeness desc, modalidad_norm/reponedor_norm/gestor_norm/supervisor_norm/rutero_norm asc, row_hash asc. source_row is NOT in the ORDER BY.
source_row is used only in exact_dup_rank (sql/11:194-197) to pick among identical row_hash duplicates (content identical), never to choose among distinct rows.
sql/11:289 ruta_person_conflict_flag from person_conflicts CTE (route_person_versions > 1).

BD-CG005B-001 is APPROVED (research/CG005B_BASTIAN_DECISIONS.json): collapse to one logical weekly grain is authorized; technical_constraint requires source_row must not select the winner and resolution must be order-independent.
Python mirror logical_winner_sort_key (loader 606-614) matches the SQL order; tests 59/62/63 assert order/source_row independence in Python.
validation_7_f07_order.residual: The SQL view's order-independence is proven by reading the ORDER BY and by the Python mirror; executing the view on the tie fixtures requires the local lab.

analysis: PostgreSQL provides pg_advisory_xact_lock(key bigint) and pg_advisory_xact_lock(key1 integer, key2 integer); there is NO pg_advisory_xact_lock(bigint, bigint).

The two-argument call therefore matches only the (integer, integer) candidate, which requires coercing both int8 args to int4; int8->int4 is an assignment cast, not implicit, so function resolution finds no candidate and raises 'function pg_advisory_xact_lock(bigint, bigint) does not exist' at execution time.
It fails closed: the lock is acquired before any write, so a failure aborts the apply/rollback with no partial commit.

The current tests use FakeCursor and only assert the SQL substring 'pg_advisory_xact_lock' and the parameters (test_56); a mocked cursor never validates the catalog signature, so the defect is hidden by the suite.
validation_8_f08_lock.required_correction: Use a single bigint key, e.g. pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(%s || '|' || %s, 0)); or use the two-int form with int4 hashes, e.g. pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(%s), hashtext(%s)) (hashtext returns int4). Add a test that executes the lock against a real local PostgreSQL.

validation_9_f09_f10_f11.apply_rejects_skip_source_check_evidence: validate_cli_args 1776-1777 raise apply_rejects_skip_source_check when args.skip_source_check under apply.
validation_9_f09_f10_f11.week_required_evidence: validate_cli_args requires --effective-week-start for apply (test_03 exit code 2); parse_effective_week_start enforces ISO + Monday.
validation_9_f09_f10_f11.no_redundant_begin_evidence: No cur.execute('BEGIN') in the loader; apply relies on autocommit=False implicit transaction (test_66 asserts absence).

validation_10_test_strength.note: No test executes against a real PostgreSQL; category counts are approximate groupings of assertion style. Several green tests assert structure (SQL substrings, POSTCHECK_CONTRACT membership, source text) rather than behavior.
assertions_not_provable_without_real_db: F-01: the resolved view actually excludes legacy batches and the 70 bajas for an explicitly-assigned week (only substring + grain-set-postcheck-name asserted).
F-02: verify_db_contract passes against the deployed view exposing cod_rt_norm.
F-03: ux_ruta_rutero_week_assignment_active enforces a single ACTIVE row and the supersede->insert cycle succeeds under real constraints.
F-04: the rollback restoration executes and (once added) the full pre-commit postcheck holds.
F-05: postcheck queries (resolved_duplicate_logical_grains_zero, resolved_grains_equal_assigned_batch_grains, no_stale_rows) run against real views.
F-06: the DB-recomputed canonical_current_surface_hash equals the Python plan hash over a real public.ruta_rutero surface.
F-07: the SQL view ORDER BY is order-independent at the database level on the tie fixtures.
F-08: pg_advisory_xact_lock(bigint,bigint) signature validity (high-confidence INVALID by catalog; needs runtime confirmation).

quality_assessment.confidence_basis: Integrity verified by executed commands (py_compile + 70/70 tests). Correction claims derived by static reading of the committed loader/SQL/tests against the prior CG005D findings. The two residual issues are definitive on inspection: F-08 is an unambiguous catalog-signature mismatch (no pg_advisory_xact_lock(bigint,bigint); int8->int4 not implicit), and F-04's missing pre-commit postcheck is visible in the rollback code path. DB-dependent behavioral proofs (F-01/F-02/F-03/F-05/F-06/F-07) are sound by construction but were not executed (no DB access authorized).
rework_items: F-08: fix advisory lock signature (single bigint key or hashtext int4 two-key form) and add a real-DB lock test.
F-04: add a full pre-commit postcheck to the rollback (restored-hash equality + weekly/resolved/grain revalidation).

next_step: ChatGPT/Bastian review: approve a local-lab phase to apply the DDL and behaviorally prove F-01/F-02/F-03/F-05/F-06/F-07, and require F-08 + F-04 corrections before any apply or rollback is trusted.
